questions.prompt: A decoder needs two bytes starting at offset i in a buffer of length n. Which check is both sufficient and resistant to unsigned wraparound?
questions.explanation: Proving i is in the closed range through n makes n - i defined, then the remaining-length check avoids wrapping i + 2. The other checks either can wrap or do not prove two bytes remain.

questions.prompt: Which expression portably decodes the little-endian bytes p[0] and p[1] as an unsigned 16-bit value?
questions.explanation: Explicit byte assembly states the wire order and avoids alignment and aliasing assumptions. A cast is not portable, C is big-endian decoding, and memcpy alone still gives host byte order.

questions.prompt: Inside a function parameter declared as char data[32], what does sizeof(data) normally evaluate as?
questions.explanation: An array parameter is adjusted to a pointer parameter. The callee must receive length separately; sizeof cannot recover the caller's array bound.

questions.prompt: An initializer allocates resource A, then allocation of B fails. What ownership rule gives the safest cleanup?

choices.A: Return immediately and let the operating system recover A
choices.B: Publish the partly initialized object and document that callers must guess what exists
choices.C: Initialize fields to an empty state and unwind every resource acquired so far

questions.explanation: A documented empty state and reverse-order unwind make partial initialization reviewable and prevent leaks or destruction of uninitialized fields.

questions.prompt: For a synchronous state machine using next_state and state, when should state normally change?

choices.B: Only on the selected clock edge or explicit reset event
choices.C: At the end of each host-language loop iteration

questions.explanation: Combinational logic computes a candidate next state; the register commits it on the defined clock edge. Mixing the two destroys cycle-level reasoning.
